name: Dorothy Mae Henderson
chief_complaint: My belly is all swollen up and I've been vomiting this green stuff. I haven't had a bowel movement in four days.
onset_description: Progressive abdominal distension and crampy pain over the past 3 days. No flatus or bowel movements for 4 days. Vomiting began 12 hours ago, initially food contents, now bilious. Pain is intermittent and colicky.

abdominal_pain.description: It comes in waves, cramps up real bad then lets up for a little bit, then comes right back

vomiting.description: I keep throwing up this green bitter stuff, can't keep anything down, not even water
vomiting.character: initially food contents, progressed to bilious (green/yellow)

abdominal_distension.description: My stomach is puffed out like a balloon, my clothes don't fit right anymore

obstipation.description: Haven't passed gas or had a bowel movement in four days, that's not normal for me
obstipation.character: complete absence of flatus and stool
alleviating_factors: tried prune juice and milk of magnesia at home with no result

labs.CBC: WBC 11.2 (mildly elevated), Hgb 11.8 (mildly low for age), Plt 210
labs.BMP: Na 132 (low - hyponatremia from vomiting), K 3.1 (low - hypokalemia from vomiting), Cr 1.4 (elevated - dehydration), Glucose 110, BUN 32 (elevated), Cl 90 (low), CO2 30 (mildly elevated - metabolic alkalosis)
labs.liver_function: AST 28, ALT 22, Alk Phos 88, Total Bilirubin 0.9 - within normal limits

past_medical_history: History of colon cancer (stage II, treated 6 years ago)
surgical_history: Open right hemicolectomy for colon cancer (2020)
medications: Acetaminophen 500mg as needed for joint pain
Calcium 600mg with Vitamin D twice daily

social_history.alcohol: Quit 20 years ago, used to drink socially
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in her own home. Daughter checks on her daily. Son lives out of state.
social_history.exercise: Limited by arthritis, uses a walker. Moves around the house.
social_history.diet: Regular diet but admits to not drinking enough water. Daughter brings meals.

personality: sweet and grandmotherly, patient and kind, but worried. Refers to the nurse as 'baby' or 'sweetheart.' Very trusting of medical staff.
communication_style: Warm and talkative, tells stories when answering questions. May need gentle redirection to stay on topic. Will mention her daughter and grandchildren. Uses expressions like 'Lord have mercy' and 'I tell you what.' Provides thorough answers when asked directly.
pain_description_style: Describes pain in waves: 'It cramps up something awful, then it eases off for a minute, then here it comes again.' Uses the phrase 'tolerable' even when in significant pain. Will pray quietly during bad cramps.

critical_findings: Complete obstipation (no flatus or stool for 4 days)
Bilious vomiting progressing from food to bile
Significant surgical history with multiple abdominal operations (adhesion risk)
Colicky/cramping pain pattern classic for mechanical bowel obstruction
Electrolyte derangements from vomiting: hyponatremia (132), hypokalemia (3.1)
Dehydration evidenced by elevated BUN/Cr ratio and concentrated urine
History of colon cancer and right hemicolectomy - adhesive SBO most likely
rubric.diagnosis: Small Bowel Obstruction (adhesive, secondary to prior abdominal surgeries)
recommended_interventions: Abdominal X-ray (KUB) - look for dilated loops and air-fluid levels
CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast (no oral contrast)
Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation with normal saline
Foley catheter for strict I&O monitoring
Serial abdominal exams to monitor for strangulation signs
